requirements.HEP-CHART-001: The eDISH scatter reduces the cleaned data to exactly one point per participant, plotting that participant's peak X-measure value against their peak Y-measure value in the active display units.
requirements.HEP-CHART-002: Each axis is titled with the selected measure's full label plus a standardization suffix ([×ULN] for eDISH, [×Baseline] for mDISH).
requirements.HEP-CHART-003: The axis domains toggle between linear and logarithmic scaling and always widen to keep the Hy's-Law cutpoint in view, with a log axis running from the smallest positive value (never 0).
requirements.HEP-CHART-004: A point's tooltip lists the participant id, R Ratio, each axis measure's standardized peak value with its study day, and the day difference between the two peaks.
requirements.HEP-QUAD-001: Default Hy's-Law cutpoints reproduce the original renderer (TB 2×ULN / 4.8×Baseline, ALP 1×ULN / 3.8×Baseline, rRatio 5/5, and a `defaults` entry of 3×ULN / 3.8×Baseline), giving the classic eDISH view ALT ≥ 3×ULN vs TB ≥ 2×ULN (and mDISH ALT 3.8 vs TB 4.8); cutFor resolves the active cutpoint for a measure+display mode, back-filling any unlisted measure or missing display mode from the `defaults` entry, and a partial cuts override deep-merges onto the defaults so untouched measures keep their cuts.
requirements.HEP-QUAD-002: Two dashed grey cut-lines at the X and Y cutpoints split the plot into the four named Hy's-Law quadrants — Possible Hy's Law Range (upper-right, High/High), Hyperbilirubinemia (upper-left, Normal/High), Temple's Corollary (lower-right, High/Normal), and Normal Range (lower-left, Normal/Normal).
requirements.HEP-QUAD-003: Each quadrant corner draws its label with the live participant percent (e.g. "Possible Hy's Law Range (20.0%)"), anchored to its corner and updating as points are reclassified.
requirements.HEP-QUAD-004: classifyQuadrants buckets each shown point into a quadrant by xCat = x ≥ xCut ? High : Normal (likewise y), so a point exactly on a cutpoint counts as High, and returns per-position counts and percent-of-shown-points (zero percents for empty data).
requirements.HEP-QUAD-005: A quadrant summary table with Quadrant | # | % columns lists all four quadrants with their live participant count and percent, staying in sync with the corner labels as cuts move.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-001: An X-axis Measure select offers the configured x_options and plots x_default (ALT) on first render.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-002: A Y-axis Measure select is rendered only when y_options has more than one entry; a single option (TB) drops the control.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-006: An Axis Type select toggles both scatter axes between linear and log.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-007: A Point Size select offers Uniform and rRatio, sizing points either at a uniform radius or scaled by the participant R-Ratio.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-008: A timing-window number input ('Highlight Points Based on Timing') sets the day threshold (default 30) that decides filled vs hollow points.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-009: A Group color-by select (always offering a leading 'None' option) colors the points by the chosen column and renders a legend; the control is dropped when only None is available.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-010: An R-Ratio range filter (min/max number inputs plus a reset) filters participants by R-Ratio, seeding a null max from the largest finite participant R-Ratio in the data.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-011: Categorical data-filter selects narrow the shown participants; filters whose column is absent from the data are dropped with a console warning.
requirements.HEP-CTRL-012: Per-axis Reference Line number inputs set the X/Y Hy's-Law cutpoints, clamped to ≥ 0, driving the quadrant classification.
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-001: The Display Type control switches the active standardization between 'Upper limit of normal adjusted (eDISH)' (÷ULN, ×ULN) and 'Baseline adjusted (mDISH)' (÷baseline, ×Baseline), re-deriving every displayed value; in mDISH the ×Baseline denominator for a participant×measure group is the study-day-0 record, else the earliest day (or the first input-order record when no day column is present).
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-002: The ×ULN (eDISH) standardization divides each result by its upper-limit-of-normal value (normal_col_high, default STNRHI).
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-003: The explorer standardizes and can plot exactly four liver measures — ALT, AST, TB, ALP — resolved from the measure column via the measure_values map, and reduces each participant to one peak record per measure (the record with the maximum value in the active display units, ×ULN or ×Baseline).
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-004: A participant with a missing or zero baseline is dropped from the mDISH scatter (its ×Baseline is NaN) and counted toward the removed-participants note.
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-005: A scatter point renders filled when its peak-X and peak-Y study days differ by no more than the timing window and hollow otherwise, where day_diff = |days_x − days_y|.
requirements.HEP-DISPLAY-006: The participant R-Ratio is the peak ALT ×ULN divided by the peak ALP ×ULN, always computed on the ULN scale regardless of display mode and NaN when either peak is missing or ALP's peak ≤ 0.
requirements.HEP-SELECT-001: Clicking a scatter point selects that participant and highlights it (selected point opaque, others dimmed).
requirements.HEP-SELECT-002: Selecting a participant opens the docked participant profile's 'Standardized Lab Values by Study Day' spaghetti — one day-ordered line per present liver measure in the active display units — and re-selecting destroys the prior profile charts before drawing the new ones so no Chart.js instance leaks (sv#98: the bespoke drawDetail chart is deleted; the dock delivers this requirement).
requirements.HEP-SELECT-003: Selecting a participant draws a visit-path trajectory overlay pairing X/Y standardized values by shared visit (else study day, else sequence) in chronological order, only where both measures are present.
requirements.HEP-SELECT-004: When no usable study day is present, day/visit ordering is derived from a per-participant-per-measure 1-based input-order sequence used to pair and order the drill-down series.
requirements.HEP-SELECT-005: Selecting a participant renders a per-measure summary table with Measure | N | Min | Median | Max of the raw (unstandardized) results for each present liver measure.
requirements.HEP-SELECT-006: Changing Display Type while a participant is selected re-renders every coordinated panel (overlay, detail chart, summary table, listing) in the new units without losing the selection.
requirements.HEP-SELECT-007: Clicking the scatter background (no point hit) clears the selection, erasing the visit-path overlay, detail panels, and linked listing.
requirements.HEP-DATA-001: Input data is long-format — one record per participant per measure per visit/day — and the module reduces it to a single plotted point per participant (settings.id_col).
requirements.HEP-DATA-002: The liver measures (ALT/AST/TB/ALP) are matched from each row's measure_col value against the full TEST strings mapped by settings.measure_values.
requirements.HEP-DATA-003: cleanData removes rows whose result is blank or non-numeric, or whose ULN is non-numeric or ≤ 0, returns the count of removed records, and that count is surfaced on-page as a warning note reading "N missing or non-numeric results removed."
requirements.HEP-DATA-004: When studyday_col is present each row's study day is parsed to numeric __hep_day (a blank or non-numeric study day becomes NaN, not coerced to 0); when no row carries a usable study day the timing test degrades gracefully — day_diff is NaN and points render filled (withinWindow true) rather than all-hollow.
requirements.HEP-DATA-005: checkInputs validates the schema's required column settings (id_col, measure_col, value_col, normal_col_high) against the remapped names and throws a single Error naming every missing variable.
requirements.HEP-API-001: The public safety.viz collection exposes a `hepExplorer` factory (identical to the named `hepExplorer` export), and the module instance exposes an init/setData/setSettings/render/resize/destroy lifecycle API where setData and setSettings return the instance for chaining and destroy empties the container.
requirements.HEP-API-003: A `participantsSelected` event fires on the root element both when a participant is selected (detail.data length 1) and when the selection is cleared (detail.data length 0).
requirements.HEP-COMP-001: Each subject is classified into one of four Hy's-Law quadrants — Normal & NN, Cholestasis, Temple's Corollary, Hy's Law (in the FDA reference's factor order) — by the strict cutpoints ALT > 3×ULN and TB > 2×ULN (the threshold value itself falls on the Normal side, per R `cut(right = TRUE)`), computed twice per subject: once from the baseline ×ULN (pretreatment) and once from the peak on-treatment ×ULN; the view draws the pretreatment and on-treatment eDISH panels with the baseline-quadrant legend.
requirements.HEP-COMP-002: The on-treatment peak is the maximum over ON-TREATMENT records only (FDA AVISITN > 0), taken independently per analyte, so baseline, screening (negative study-day), and duplicate day-0 records are excluded; an on-treatment value exactly at a cutpoint (e.g. BILI 2×ULN) is not elevated (strict >).
requirements.HEP-COMP-003: Each subject's ×Baseline (×BLN) value is the peak on-treatment value divided by that subject's own baseline value, and the peak ×ULN and peak ×BLN are independent maxima (ULN can vary across records); these feed the four-panel ×Baseline shift plot.
requirements.HEP-COMP-004: A migration table counts pretreatment × on-treatment quadrant migrations (with row totals, column totals, and grand total) and colors each cell by the FDA 4×4 DILI-concern matrix — red (migration of concern / worsening), yellow (potential concern / lateral single-analyte), green (no concern / potential benefit / improvement toward Normal), gray (no migration, the diagonal): 5 red, 2 yellow, 5 green, 4 gray.
requirements.HEP-COMP-005: A by-arm summary buckets each subject's migration by concern color per treatment arm (using the group/arm meta carried on each subject), collapsing to a single "all subjects" row when no arm column is present.
requirements.HEP-COMP-006: The renderer opens on the composite view with a reduced control set, a View control toggles between the composite and scatter views, subjects lacking an on-treatment ALT/BILI peak are excluded, and the view degrades gracefully when baseline or on-treatment values are absent.
requirements.HEP-COMP-007: Hovering or clicking a point traces and multi-selects that participant across every composite panel (each panel carries an id header for the cross-linking).
requirements.HEP-CORE-001: buildHepSubjects reduces the cleaned long-format records to exactly one object per participant carrying, for both ALT and total bilirubin, that participant's baseline value and their maximum on-treatment value in both ×ULN and the data's absolute units, from a single reduction shared by every hepatic figure.
requirements.HEP-CORE-002: The maximum on-treatment value excludes the resolved baseline record by object identity, not by a study-day test alone, so a participant whose highest value is their own baseline — including a baseline recorded at an unscheduled positive study day — yields an on-treatment maximum strictly below baseline.
requirements.HEP-CORE-003: The baseline record is resolved in a fixed precedence: the record whose baseline_col equals baseline_value when both settings are supplied, otherwise the study-day-0 record, otherwise the earliest record ordered by study day and then by input order.
requirements.HEP-CORE-004: The absolute maximum is taken over the raw result independently of the ×ULN maximum, so when a participant's reference range varies across visits the two peaks may fall on different records and neither is derived from the other.
requirements.HEP-CORE-005: The column named by settings.arm_col is retained on every subject's metadata unconditionally, so the arm value survives the reduction without having to be registered as a grouping column or as a data filter.
requirements.HEP-CORE-006: newOnsetJaundice is true for a participant when their baseline total bilirubin is at or below the jaundice_uln threshold in ×ULN and their maximum on-treatment total bilirubin is strictly above it, and false otherwise, including when either value is missing.
requirements.HEP-CORE-007: resolveArmSides returns a map from every distinct arm value present in the data to placebo, active or null, pooling every non-placebo arm into active when active_arms is null and mapping to null any arm that an explicit active_arms list omits.
requirements.HEP-CORE-008: shiftDirection and concernOf agree across all sixteen baseline × on-treatment quadrant pairs: every pair concernOf calls red is up, every green pair is down, and every yellow or gray pair is lateral.
requirements.HEP-CORE-009: boxStats returns n, min, q5, q25, median, q75, q95, mean and max for a list of values, using the R-7 interpolated quantile definition already shipped in the library.
requirements.HEP-CORE-010: The promoted shared box-and-whisker draw renders marks identical to the pre-promotion results-over-time implementation for the same specs and scales.
requirements.HEP-CORE-011: The view-agnostic selection layer fills the Participants control with one option per shown participant and hides the control when no participants are shown.
requirements.HEP-CORE-012: Syncing the selection layer mirrors the current participant selection into both the Participants dropdown and the Clear button state.
requirements.HEP-CORE-013: The selection layer routes change and clear events to whichever view is currently bound, holding no view-conditional logic of its own.
requirements.HEP-CORE-014: Dispatching a selection fires the participantsSelected event on the shell root, and the carried-selection helper replays that selection across a redraw.
requirements.HEP-CORE-015: The shared trace header renders distinct text for a hover, a single selection, several selections, and the idle hint.
requirements.HEP-CORE-016: The selection layer contains no view-conditional logic and imports no view module.
requirements.HEP-CORE-017: No file under src/hep-explorer/views/ imports another file in views/, so the views stay independent siblings.
requirements.HEP-CORE-018: Only the orchestrator (src/hep-explorer.js) branches on state.view; no shared widget does.
requirements.HEP-CORE-019: Every view implements the same five-method view contract against the same shared shell.
requirements.HEP-MIG-001: The migration view renders three node columns, with each participant's pretreatment (baseline) categorization in the centre column and their maximum on-treatment categorization in one of the two flanking columns.
requirements.HEP-MIG-002: Placebo-arm ribbons extend leftward from the centre column and active-arm ribbons extend rightward, so the two arms read as mirror images about the shared baseline spine.
requirements.HEP-MIG-003: Within every column the quadrant nodes are stacked in severity order from top to bottom — Hy's Law highest and Normal & NN lowest — so vertical position carries the same meaning in all three columns.
requirements.HEP-MIG-004: Cholestasis and Temple's Corollary occupy one shared severity tier, drawn as two adjacent sub-nodes beneath a single tier label, because the concern matrix declines to rank either above the other.
requirements.HEP-MIG-005: Node height is proportional to the node's participant count on a single pixels-per-participant scale, and a quadrant with a zero count still renders as a one-pixel stub with a dimmed label so the four-row grid stays geometrically stable as filters change.
requirements.HEP-MIG-006: A centre node's height is the larger of its two arms' baseline counts, and each face allocates only its own arm's count centred within that height, so a shortfall reads as symmetric padding on the face of the arm with fewer participants.
requirements.HEP-MIG-007: Ribbon thickness is proportional to participant count on the same pixels-per-participant scale as node heights, and ribbons are painted thickest first so a thin ribbon is never occluded by, and stays clickable over, a thicker one.
requirements.HEP-MIG-008: Ribbon fill colour is derived from concernOf applied to the baseline and on-treatment quadrant pair, and never from the vertical direction the ribbon happens to travel.
requirements.HEP-MIG-009: Rendered ribbon geometry matches its concern class for every populated flow: a red ribbon ends strictly above where it starts, a green ribbon ends strictly below, and yellow and gray ribbons run horizontally.
requirements.HEP-MIG-010: Node fill colours are taken from QUADRANT_STYLE, so a quadrant is drawn in the same colour in the migration view as in the composite view.
requirements.HEP-MIG-011: Each node label carries the quadrant name together with its participant count for each arm, so the counts can be read without measuring the geometry.
requirements.HEP-MIG-012: A diagonal flow, where the baseline and on-treatment quadrants are the same, renders as a straight horizontal band between the two vertically aligned tiers rather than as a loop.
requirements.HEP-MIG-013: A Hide unchanged control removes the diagonal ribbons from the plot and reports the number of participants they represent in the notes, leaving the cross tables unchanged.
requirements.HEP-MIG-014: The migration view renders both an svg plot and at least one table inside the main column.
requirements.HEP-MIG-015: The computed node and ribbon geometry, together with the shared pixels-per-participant scale, is exposed on the root element as $hepSankey.
requirements.HEP-MIG-016: Every rendered coordinate is rounded to an integer before serialisation, so two renders of the same cohort produce byte-identical path data.
requirements.HEP-MIG-017: Hovering a ribbon highlights that ribbon and both of its endpoint nodes and opens a positioned HTML tooltip stating the flow's participant count, its two quadrants and its arm; the tooltip is an HTML element rather than an SVG title so it appears in evidence screenshots.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-001: One cross table is rendered per designated arm, with rows for the baseline quadrant and columns for the on-treatment quadrant, both ordered by severity so the tables read in the same direction as the plot.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-002: Each cross table carries a total column, a total row, and a grand total.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-003: Each cross-table cell is shaded by concernOf using the four-level CONCERN_COLORS, and a legend names the four concern levels.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-004: Every cross-table cell count equals the participant count of the ribbon with the same arm, baseline quadrant and on-treatment quadrant.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-005: Clicking a cross-table cell produces exactly the same participant selection as clicking its corresponding ribbon.
requirements.HEP-XTAB-006: The composite view's existing pooled migration table keeps its existing content and ordering; the migration view's per-arm tables are additional and do not alter it.
requirements.HEP-STEP-001: Clicking a ribbon populates the Participants control with that flow's participants and dispatches a participantsSelected event carrying exactly their identifiers.
requirements.HEP-STEP-002: With a flow selected, the footnote states the shift, the arm it belongs to and its participant count, and offers a control to review those participants in the composite plot.
requirements.HEP-STEP-003: Activating that control switches to the composite view with exactly those participants restored as the selection and highlighted in the ×Baseline panels.
requirements.HEP-STEP-004: A participant selection made in any of the three views survives a switch to either of the other two, for every ordered pair of views.
requirements.HEP-STEP-005: When the Hy's Law to Hy's Law cell is non-empty, the view renders a caution note reporting the count, stating that a shift view cannot detect worsening within a category, and offering to select those participants for individual review.
requirements.HEP-ARM-001: The arm column is resolved from settings.arm_col when that column is present in the data, and otherwise auto-detected as the first of ARM, ACTARM, TRT01A, TREATMENT and TRTA that is present.
requirements.HEP-ARM-002: When placebo_arm is null, the placebo arm is auto-detected as the first arm value matching a case-insensitive placebo-or-control pattern.
requirements.HEP-ARM-003: Explicit placebo_arm and active_arms settings take precedence over auto-detection whenever the values they name are present in the data.
requirements.HEP-ARM-004: Participants whose arm is designated neither placebo nor active are excluded from the migration view and their count is reported in the notes.
requirements.HEP-ARM-005: When fewer than two arms resolve to opposite sides, the Sankey renders unidirectionally with a warning note rather than throwing.
requirements.HEP-ARM-006: When no arm column can be resolved at all, the Migration view option is disabled with an explanatory tooltip rather than offering a view that cannot be drawn.
requirements.HEP-ARM-007: Arm designation scopes the migration view's cohort only; the scatter and composite views continue to plot the full cohort regardless of which arms are designated.
requirements.HEP-ARM-008: When several active arms pool into the single active side, the pooled arms are named in an on-page note directing the reviewer to the Active-arm control to compare one at a time.
requirements.HEP-ACC-001: Every ribbon is focusable, carries role="button", and has an accessible name stating the participant count, both quadrants, the arm and the concern direction.
requirements.HEP-ACC-002: A focused ribbon is activated by both Enter and Space, producing the same participant selection as a pointer click.
requirements.HEP-ACC-003: The Sankey svg carries role="img" and an accessible name summarising the unfavourable and favourable shift counts for each arm.
requirements.HEP-DATA-011: Records dropped because their reference range is missing or non-positive are counted and reported in the notes separately from records dropped for a missing or non-numeric result.
requirements.HEP-DATA-012: The migration view reports the count of participants excluded for lacking a usable baseline or a usable on-treatment measurement, separately from the count excluded for an undesignated arm.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-001: Hy's-Law cut-lines are draggable so users can interactively reposition the ALT and TB quadrant thresholds and recompute the quadrant summary.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-002: A study-day animation with a play control advances points over time and renders motion trails showing each participant's path.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-003: Marginal distribution box plots and axis rugs are drawn alongside the x- and y-axes to summarize the univariate ALT and TB distributions.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-004: The participant lab summary table renders per-measure sparklines and supports drill-down from a sparkline into that measure's detail.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-005: An exposure (EX) track overlays drug-exposure intervals in the participant detail and reports a P_ALT (probability of ALT elevation) estimate.
requirements.HEP-DEFER-006: CSV downloads are offered for dropped records and participants, and below-LLOQ values are imputed during data cleaning.
